# UC-22.42.1 · Unauthorized production change — no approved CR matches the observed change

## Description

Validates every production change event against the CR database in real time. Any change without an approved CR is an immediate ITGC deficiency — and a potential security event.

## Value

Replaces the standard quarterly-sample audit with continuous evidence of the authorisation gate.

## Implementation

(1) Force CI/CD tools to emit change events tagged with change_id; (2) ingest CR state from the ITSM platform; (3) run hourly; (4) on finding, escalate via SOAR. Indexes required: `index=change`, `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=prod:change`, `sourcetype=servicenow:change`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: See subcategory preamble..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Force CI/CD tools to emit change events tagged with change_id; (2) ingest CR state from the ITSM platform; (3) run hourly; (4) on finding, escalate via SOAR.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=change sourcetype=prod:change earliest=-1h
| join type=outer change_id [search index=itsm sourcetype=servicenow:change earliest=-1h state=approved | fields change_id approved_at]
| where isnull(approved_at)
| table _time change_id system applied_by system_owner
```

Understanding this SPL

**Unauthorized production change — no approved CR matches the observed change** — Replaces the standard quarterly-sample audit with continuous evidence of the authorisation gate.

Documented **Data sources**: See subcategory preamble. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: change; **sourcetype**: prod:change. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=change, sourcetype=prod:change, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(approved_at)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: _time, applied_by, change_id, system, system_owner.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with change or CMDB source-of-truth and compare with the GRC system so baseline drift matches approved changes.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked bar approved vs unauthorised by system, table of findings, single-value '% authorised changes'.

## SPL

```spl
index=change sourcetype=prod:change earliest=-1h
| join type=outer change_id [search index=itsm sourcetype=servicenow:change earliest=-1h state=approved | fields change_id approved_at]
| where isnull(approved_at)
| table _time change_id system applied_by system_owner
```

## Visualization

Stacked bar approved vs unauthorised by system, table of findings, single-value '% authorised changes'.
